LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT SANTA BARBARA SAN LUIS OBISPO REGIONAL HEALTH'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED ON THE GROUNDS THAT 42 C.F.R. 435.1015 IS AN ENACTMENT WHICH IMPOSES A MANDATORY DUTY NOT TO BUY INSURANCE UNDER A HIPP PROGRAM WHERE THE COST OF PROVIDING COVERAGE DIRECTLY IS SUBSTANTIALLY MORE THAN THE COST OF BUYING THE INSURANCE. THE COMPLAINT OF BLUE SHIELD ALLEGES THAT AS A VIOLATION AND AS A BASIS FOR THEIR SOLE CLAIM UNDER 815.6. COUNSEL FOR BLUE SHIELD TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT REFLECTS THE COURT'S RULING AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Plaintiff Blue Shield Of Californias Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-15-2018 Continued To Jun-13-18 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances :
Atty. Gregory Pimstone, Atty. Ronald B. Turovsky & Atty. Steve Chiunof Manatt, Phelps & Phillips, LLP, Tel. No. 1-310-312-4249 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Caroline Brown of Covington & Ruzling, Tel. No. 1-202-667-5219 appearing for the defendant;
Atty. William Clinenbeard of Clinkenbeard & Ramsey, Tel. No. 1-805-965-0043 appearing
for the defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; Plaintiff California Physicians' Service's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is continued to June 13 2018 at 1:30 p.m, in Dept. 302. But for the Government Code 810.8 issue which the court is deferring to the June 13 hearing, Blue Shield has shown that it is entitled to judgment in its favor. 42 CFR 435.1015 creates a mandatory duty on CenCal not to make premium assistance payments where the cost of the premiums is substantially less expensive than the cost of providing direct coverage by CenCal. For purposes of Government Code 815.6, this mandatory duty is imposed by an enactment designed to protect private insurers such as Blue Shield from having an imbalanced risk pool with a disproportionately high number of severely ill insureds. The undisputed facts establish that CenCal violated this mandatory duty by paying premiums to Blue Shield for 40 of its Medi-Cal beneficiaries with end-stage renal failure who had not previously been insured by Blue Shield because the cost of providing direct coverage for those beneficiaries far exceeded the premiums. No later than May 25, 2018 defendant Santa Barbara San Luis Obispo Regional Health Authority may file a supplemental opposition memorandum limited to the issue of whether if the same premium assistance payments paid by CenCal were paid by a private health care organization those payments are actionable per the Unfair Competition Law, Business and Professions Code 17200, for purposes of Government Code 810.8. (See, e.g., Cel-Tech Communications, Inc. v. Los Angeles Cellular Telephone Co. (1999) 20 Cal. 4th 163). No later than June 5, 2018 Blue Shield may file a supplemental reply memorandum limited to the same issue. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-14-2016 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gregory Pimstone, Ronald Turovsky and Steve Chiu of Manatt, Phelps & Phillips, LLP telephone number 310-312-4133 for Plaintiff California Physicians' Service dba Blue Shield of California.
William Clinkenbeard of Clinkerbeard, Ramsey, Spackman & Clark, LLP telephone number 805-965-0043 appeared by telephone for Defendant Santa Barbara San Luis Obispo Regional Health Authority dba CenCal Health.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, DEFENDANT SANTA BARBARA SAN LUIS OBISPO REGIONAL HEALTH'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled on the grounds that 42 C.F.R. 435.1015 is an enactment which imposes a mandatory duty not to buy insurance under a HIPP program where the cost of providing coverage directly is substantially more than the cost of buying the insurance. The complaint of Blue Shield alleges that as a violation and as a basis for their sole claim under 815.6. Counsel for Blue Shield to prepare a form of order that reflects the Court's ruling and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
